div1.entryFree:
O'ersway, 1) to control, to rule, to lord over: so pertaunt-like would I o. his state, that he should be my fool and I his fate, LLL V, 2, 67. if he be so resolved, I can o. him, Caes. II, 1, 203.
2) to surpass in power: sad mortality --s their power, Sonn. 65, 2. great command --s the order, Hml. V, 1, 251.
